behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary (CSV parser -> schema validator -> DLQ writer): row-number metadata can be lost or remapped even if each function works alone (MOCK-MISS=yes). No real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce this specific failure if DLQ is tested via an in-process adapter/fake sink (REAL-INFRA=no for this behavior). No AI output dependency (LLM-DEP=no) and no multi-agent sequence (MULTI-STEP=no). Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires correct rejection and row-number propagation, not real queue transport/network semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline modules together on a small CSV fixture with known missing required fields and assert rejected rows are emitted to the DLQ adapter with exact original row numbers preserved.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is pure transformation logic on input strings and does not require module handoff, I/O, OS/network, or subprocess behavior (MOCK-MISS=no, REAL-INFRA=no). A single function/object boundary test can catch real parsing/normalization failures. No AI dependency or multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected higher level: Integration is disqualified because behavior text is only about conversion correctness, not orchestration between components.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for the date-normalization function with ISO 8601, US, European, ambiguous, and invalid examples; assert canonical output or explicit validation error.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on runtime memory pressure, allocator/GC behavior, and timeout under realistic process execution (REAL-INFRA=yes). In-process unit/integration fakes typically hide these failures (MOCK-HIDE=yes). No AI dependency, no agentic sequencing. Rejected lower level: Integration is insufficient because behavior text explicitly requires timeout and memory characteristics at large scale, which are OS/process-level effects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a 500k-row fixture, enforce configured timeout, and assert peak memory stays below budget and run completes successfully.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real HTTP error handling and retry timing behavior between the pipeline and S3 client stack (REAL-INFRA=yes). Mocked clients can mask retry-policy wiring, status-code interpretation, and backoff timing behavior (MOCK-HIDE=yes). No AI dependency or multi-agent chain. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified because behavior text explicitly references S3 transient 503 handling and backoff behavior under real HTTP interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an HTTP endpoint (or S3-compatible test service) that returns 503 for initial attempts then succeeds; assert exponential backoff intervals and batch eventual success without full-batch failure.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: This targets crash/restart recovery across process boundary plus persisted DB/checkpoint state, which is subprocess/OS + real DB interaction (REAL-INFRA=yes). Mocking or single-process simulation can miss transactional/idempotency edge cases that appear on actual restart (MOCK-HIDE=yes). No AI dependency or multi-agent pipeline. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified by behavior text 'restarted mid-batch', which requires validating correctness across a real restart boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start pipeline on a fixture, force stop mid-batch, restart process with same input and checkpoint state against PostgreSQL, then assert final table has exactly-once rows (no duplicates) and expected count.
